topic: Knowledge Graph Viewers — VOKB and VKB
summary:
## Purpose
Browser-based interactive visualizations of the operational knowledge graph for exploring entities, relations, and graph topology. Three implementations exist with distinct tech stacks and ontology scopes.

## Architecture
**VOKB:**
- Root: `rapid-automations/integrations/operational-knowledge-management/`
- Stack: React 18 + D3.js + Vite + Tailwind CSS; served on port 3002 via Docker/supervisord

**VKB (sigma.js, legacy):**
- Stack: sigma.js; served on port 8080
- Port 8080 may conflict with `coding-services` health check endpoint — verify before starting

**Unified Viewer (`integrations/unified-viewer/src/`):**
- Stack: React + D3 force-simulation canvas; Zustand for filter state; serves VOKB, VKB, and OKB scopes through a single app
- `D3GraphCanvas.tsx`: `hiddenRelationTypes`/`hiddenNodeTypes` filter memos enforce layer separation; per-edge color lookup via `EDGE_STYLES` (keys must be lowercase/camelCase)
- `relation-types.ts`: viewer-side canonicalizer collapses space/underscore variant pairs before render to prevent duplicate edges
- Filter infrastructure: `viewer-store.ts` (Zustand) + `FilterRail.tsx`, `GraphToggles.tsx`, `TrendingPanel.tsx`, `LegendPanel.tsx`
- `integrations/unified-viewer` is a **regular tracked subdirectory** — not a git submodule

## Key Files
- `rapid-automations/integrations/operational-knowledge-management/` — VOKB application root
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/D3GraphCanvas.tsx` — D3 canvas; layer separation; per-edge color lookup
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/relation-types.ts` — relation-type canonicalizer
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/vokb-palette.ts` — `EDGE_STYLES` palette definitions
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/store/viewer-store.ts` — Zustand filter store
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/api/ApiClient.ts` — API client for graph entity and relation data

## Usage
- VOKB: Docker/supervisord on port 3002; React+D3 stack
- VKB (sigma.js): port 8080; start only after confirming no port conflict with `coding-services`
- Unified Viewer: active ontology scope determines which filter controls are visible (e.g., Layer filter is suppressed for coding-system graphs); all three scopes share the same React app and Zustand store

## Troubleshooting
- **`integrations/unified-viewer` shows unexpected git submodule state**: it is a regular tracked subdirectory — use standard git staging, not submodule commands
- **VOKB port 3002 unreachable**: check Docker and supervisord; restart via `supervisorctl restart vokb`
- **VKB port 8080 conflict**: stop `coding-services` health check before starting the sigma.js VKB server
- **Edge rendering or duplicate-edge issues in Unified Viewer**: see the "VKB Graph Viewer — Ontology Bleed and Entity Integrity" insight for detailed diagnostics on palette key format and canonicalizer coverage

metadata.topic: Knowledge Graph Viewers — VOKB and VKB
metadata.summary:
## Purpose
Browser-based interactive visualizations of the operational knowledge graph for exploring entities, relations, and graph topology. Three implementations exist with distinct tech stacks and ontology scopes.

## Architecture
**VOKB:**
- Root: `rapid-automations/integrations/operational-knowledge-management/`
- Stack: React 18 + D3.js + Vite + Tailwind CSS; served on port 3002 via Docker/supervisord

**VKB (sigma.js, legacy):**
- Stack: sigma.js; served on port 8080
- Port 8080 may conflict with `coding-services` health check endpoint — verify before starting

**Unified Viewer (`integrations/unified-viewer/src/`):**
- Stack: React + D3 force-simulation canvas; Zustand for filter state; serves VOKB, VKB, and OKB scopes through a single app
- `D3GraphCanvas.tsx`: `hiddenRelationTypes`/`hiddenNodeTypes` filter memos enforce layer separation; per-edge color lookup via `EDGE_STYLES` (keys must be lowercase/camelCase)
- `relation-types.ts`: viewer-side canonicalizer collapses space/underscore variant pairs before render to prevent duplicate edges
- Filter infrastructure: `viewer-store.ts` (Zustand) + `FilterRail.tsx`, `GraphToggles.tsx`, `TrendingPanel.tsx`, `LegendPanel.tsx`
- `integrations/unified-viewer` is a **regular tracked subdirectory** — not a git submodule

## Key Files
- `rapid-automations/integrations/operational-knowledge-management/` — VOKB application root
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/D3GraphCanvas.tsx` — D3 canvas; layer separation; per-edge color lookup
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/relation-types.ts` — relation-type canonicalizer
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/graph/vokb-palette.ts` — `EDGE_STYLES` palette definitions
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/store/viewer-store.ts` — Zustand filter store
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/api/ApiClient.ts` — API client for graph entity and relation data

## Usage
- VOKB: Docker/supervisord on port 3002; React+D3 stack
- VKB (sigma.js): port 8080; start only after confirming no port conflict with `coding-services`
- Unified Viewer: active ontology scope determines which filter controls are visible (e.g., Layer filter is suppressed for coding-system graphs); all three scopes share the same React app and Zustand store

## Troubleshooting
- **`integrations/unified-viewer` shows unexpected git submodule state**: it is a regular tracked subdirectory — use standard git staging, not submodule commands
- **VOKB port 3002 unreachable**: check Docker and supervisord; restart via `supervisorctl restart vokb`
- **VKB port 8080 conflict**: stop `coding-services` health check before starting the sigma.js VKB server
- **Edge rendering or duplicate-edge issues in Unified Viewer**: see the "VKB Graph Viewer — Ontology Bleed and Entity Integrity" insight for detailed diagnostics on palette key format and canonicalizer coverage
metadata.scope: All three knowledge-graph viewer implementations: VOKB (port 3002), sigma.js VKB (port 8080), and Unified Viewer

topic: Multi-Agent Skill Architecture — Skill Discovery and Distribution
summary:
## Purpose
Manages how skills (task-specific instruction sets) are installed, symlinked, and discovered across five coding agents. Two coexisting skill ecosystems serve different agent populations through distinct installation mechanisms and instruction formats.

## Architecture
**Two-tier skill ecosystem:**
- **GSD skills** (`~/.claude/get-shit-done/`): v1.34.2; 69 skill directories, 24 sub-agents, 9 lifecycle hooks, 68 workflow definitions; manifest-based self-update tracked in `~/.cache/gsd/`; `SKILL.md` files use XML-style delegation to external workflow files via `@$HOME` references; **Claude Code-exclusive** — not distributed to other agents
- **Resi skills** (`~/Agentic/_work/resi/skills/`): 6 team-level skills (`codecraft-zuul-api`, `ddad-rpu`, `fix-vulnerabilities`, `get-codeowner`, `raas-api`, `s3-session-search`); self-contained `SKILL.md` with inline Markdown; installed across agents via a Python `link` script — the **only cross-agent installer**

**Skill distribution per agent:**
- `~/.claude/skills/`: **75 entries** = 69 GSD real directories + 6 Resi symlinks
- `~/.copilot/skills/` and `~/.codex/skills/`: **6 Resi symlinks only** — GSD excluded
- **OpenCode** (`~/.config/opencode/`): no native skill directory; reads `CLAUDE.md` directly; GSD path referenced in permissions config but directory does not yet exist
- **Mastra** (`~/.mastracode/`): independent DB-based observation pipeline with lifecycle hooks; no skill system
- **Copilot**: used only as an LLM provider via OpenCode — no standalone agent config

**Cross-agent instruction synthesis:**
- `generate-agent-instructions.sh` translates 3 project-level skills (`documentation-style`, `playwright-cli`, `session-log-loader`) into agent-native formats: slash commands for Claude, `instructions.md` injection for Copilot, `CLAUDE.md` append for OpenCode
- `launch-agent-common.sh` (394 lines, 19 steps): orchestrates Docker transitions, network detection, and per-agent startup

**Known issue:** Symlink resolution for Resi skills uses a layered symlink-within-symlink structure; `readlink` must be invoked without trailing slashes — trailing slashes produce incorrect or conflicting targets.

## Key Files
- `~/.claude/skills/` — 75 total entries (69 GSD directories + 6 Resi symlinks)
- `~/.claude/get-shit-done/` — GSD skill engine root; `~/.claude/gsd-file-manifest.json` for self-update manifest
- `~/Agentic/_work/resi/skills/` — Resi skills source repository
- `~/Agentic/_work/resi/skills/link` — Python installer; symlinks Resi skills into `~/.claude/`, `~/.copilot/`, `~/.codex/`
- `~/.config/opencode/config.json` — OpenCode config; references GSD paths in permissions block
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — Copilot instruction injection point
- `generate-agent-instructions.sh` — cross-agent instruction synthesis script
- `launch-agent-common.sh` — 394-line, 19-step agent launch orchestrator

## Usage
GSD skills are installed to `~/.claude/skills/` via the GSD self-update mechanism. Resi skills are installed across all agents by running `~/Agentic/_work/resi/skills/link`. After adding or modifying project-level skills, re-run `generate-agent-instructions.sh` to propagate changes to all agents. OpenCode skill visibility depends entirely on `CLAUDE.md` content — no skill directory is scanned at runtime.

## Troubleshooting
- **New GSD skill not visible in Claude Code**: Verify directory exists in `~/.claude/skills/`; check `~/.claude/gsd-file-manifest.json` is current; trigger a GSD self-update if stale
- **Resi skill not visible in Copilot or Codex**: Re-run `~/Agentic/_work/resi/skills/link`; verify `~/.copilot/skills/` and `~/.codex/skills/` exist
- **OpenCode not picking up skills**: OpenCode reads `CLAUDE.md` only — re-run `generate-agent-instructions.sh` to inject updated skill catalog
- **Symlink resolution conflict**: Use `readlink` without trailing slashes; trailing slashes produce incorrect targets in the layered symlink structure
- **`~/.config/opencode/get-shit-done/` not found**: Path referenced in OpenCode permissions config but directory not yet created — current workaround is `CLAUDE.md` injection
- **Workspace `CLAUDE.md` not reflecting latest project-level skills**: Re-run `generate-agent-instructions.sh`

summary:
## Purpose
v7.5 formalizes a Cross-Agent Comparison Experiment Runner orchestrating variant-based A/B testing across `claude`, `copilot`, `opencode`, and `mastra`. Enables empirical, repeatable side-by-side agent performance comparison on identical tasks. All v7.5 goals wire to existing v7.4 Performance Measurement System primitives — no greenfield measurement infrastructure required.

## Architecture
Active state tracked in `STATE.md`. **14 requirements** across 5 categories in `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md`.

| Phase | Name | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 76 | Measurement Validity Fixes | CLOSED |
| 77 | Experiment Spec & Per-Variant Snapshot Foundation | CLOSED |
| 78 | Autonomous Cross-Agent Runner Architecture | COMPLETE |
| 79 | (next phase) | Unblocked |

**Phase 78 implementation:**
- `lib/experiments/agent-headless.mjs`: `argvForAgent`, `resolveAgentBinary`, `probeCopilotHeadless (-p --allow-all-tools)`; all four agents confirmed viable for headless invocation; `argvForAgent` for opencode **must** include the auto-approve flag
- `lib/experiments/experiment-runner.mjs` (~427 lines): exports `runMatrix`, `runTask`, and a terminal-state machine; handles normal, skip, and terminal-state outcomes; run-record schema includes `variant`, `repeat`, and composite `task_id` (`exp-{context}-{condition}-{model}-{run}`)

**Matrix run validation:**
- Successful matrix runs exit with code 0
- Run logs written to `scratchpad/compare-fizzbuzz-run.log` (fizzbuzz comparison experiment is the canonical validation case)
- Inspect logs with grep patterns `writeRun`, `writeScore`, `clos*` to confirm per-cell completion and terminal states across the matrix

**Known opencode headless behavioral issue:**
- Opencode exhibits a ~6.5-minute hang in a stuck approval/retry state when the auto-approve flag is absent from `argv`
- Symptom signature: ~112 calls over 13 minutes, then repeating ~9-input/4-output cycles every ~65 seconds — not an infinite loop, but a stalled retry cadence
- Fix: ensure `argvForAgent` for opencode includes the auto-approve flag; validate via opencode argv test assertions

## Key Files
- `STATE.md` — active milestone tracker
- `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` — 14 requirements across 5 categories
- `lib/experiments/agent-headless.mjs` — headless agent invocation; `argvForAgent` must include auto-approve flag for opencode
- `lib/experiments/experiment-runner.mjs` — matrix runner: `runMatrix`, `runTask`, terminal-state machine
- `scratchpad/compare-fizzbuzz-run.log` — fizzbuzz comparison experiment run log; canonical matrix validation artifact

## Usage
`runMatrix` (from `experiment-runner.mjs`) is the primary API; also exposed via an operator-facing CLI. Runs are triggered across all variant/agent/model combinations; records are persisted for all outcome types. Scores are written via the scoring pipeline on close. Post-run validation: check exit code 0, then grep the run log with `writeRun`/`writeScore`/`clos*`.

## Troubleshooting
- **Opencode headless hang (~6.5 min)**: add the auto-approve flag to opencode's `argv` in `argvForAgent`; fix opencode argv test assertions as the immediate unblocking step
- **Matrix run validation failure**: exit code non-zero or missing `writeRun`/`writeScore` entries in log indicates incomplete cell execution; re-run after confirming agent argv flags and proxy routing are correct
- **Missing token capture across matrix cells**: proxy routing regression — all agents must route through the shared proxy; see Agent Proxy Routing — Launcher Integration insight
- **Phase 79 prerequisites**: phases 76–78 all closed/complete; no outstanding measurement primitive dependencies

topic: macOS Quarantine Auto-Removal for Claude Code Binary
summary:
## Purpose
Prevents macOS Gatekeeper quarantine from blocking the Claude Code binary after Homebrew Cask upgrades, manual reinstalls, or auto-updates. A persistent `launchd` agent silently removes the quarantine extended attribute whenever the binary is modified, creating a self-healing setup that requires no user intervention.

## Architecture
- **Immediate removal**: `xattr -d com.<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED>.quarantine <claude-binary-path>` strips the quarantine attribute on demand — sufficient to unblock a currently-blocked binary without reinstall.
- **Persistent agent**: A `launchd` plist with a `WatchPaths` key targeting the Claude binary fires the `xattr` removal command whenever the file is written (upgrade, reinstall, or auto-update event).
- **Coverage**: All update vectors are handled — `brew upgrade claude-code` (Homebrew Cask), manual binary replacement, and any in-app auto-update mechanism — without shell wrapper shims or per-session manual intervention.

## Key Files
- `~/Library/LaunchAgents/<agent-label>.plist` — launchd user agent; contains `WatchPaths` pointing at the Claude binary and a `ProgramArguments` stanza running `xattr -d com.<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED>.quarantine`
- Claude binary path — typically `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude` (<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED> Silicon Homebrew) or `/usr/local/bin/claude` (<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED> Homebrew)

## Usage
- Load once after creating the plist: `launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/<agent-label>.plist`
- The agent is active at every login and fires automatically on each binary modification; no further maintenance is needed.
- Applies to the `rapid-automations` project environment and any macOS machine where Claude Code is managed via Homebrew Cask.

## Troubleshooting
- **Binary still blocked after upgrade**: Confirm the quarantine attribute is present with `xattr -l $(which claude)`; strip manually with `xattr -d com.<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED>.quarantine $(which claude)` as an immediate fix while diagnosing the agent.
- **Agent not firing**: Check it is loaded via `launchctl list | grep <agent-label>`; inspect errors with `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED>.launchd"' --last 5m`.
- **Agent unloaded after reboot**: Ensure the plist is in `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` (not `/tmp` or a project directory) and has correct ownership (`chown $USER`).
- **Wrong binary path**: If Claude is installed via a non-standard prefix, locate the real binary with `which claude` or `brew --prefix`/`bin/claude` and update `WatchPaths` accordingly.

metadata.scope: launchd agent that monitors the Claude Code binary and strips quarantine extended attributes on every update

summary:
## Purpose
`coding/bin/coding` is the primary agent runner entry point for the `coding` project. It resolves the `claude` CLI binary via `PATH`, which is supplied by the `claude-code` Homebrew cask. Incomplete cask upgrades can leave a dangling symlink that silently breaks `coding/bin/coding` with no clear error message pointing at the root cause.

## Architecture
- **Resolution chain**: `coding/bin/coding` → `claude` lookup via `PATH` → `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude` (symlink managed by Homebrew) → `/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/claude-code/<version>/claude` (actual binary)
- **Failure mode — incomplete cask extraction**: A Homebrew cask upgrade updates the `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude` symlink to point at the new version directory but may fail to extract the binary into `/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/claude-code/<version>/`, leaving a dangling symlink; `coding/bin/coding` then fails to start because the resolved path does not exist
- **Manual symlink fix is insufficient**: The binary file itself is absent, not merely misdirected; correcting the symlink target without a reinstall still yields a missing file

## Key Files
- `coding/bin/coding` — agent runner entry point; requires `claude` on `PATH`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude` — Homebrew-managed symlink; inspect with `ls -la /opt/homebrew/bin/claude`
- `/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/claude-code/<version>/` — cask extraction directory; must contain the actual `claude` binary after a successful install

## Usage
`coding/bin/coding` is invoked directly to start any agent session. It must be able to exec `claude` before any session logic runs. The dependency is implicit (via `PATH`) rather than an explicit configured path.

## Troubleshooting
- **`coding/bin/coding` fails to start after a `claude-code` upgrade**: Run `ls -la $(which claude)` to check for a dangling symlink, then `ls /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/claude-code/` to confirm the binary is missing from the new version directory
- **Fix — dangling symlink after incomplete cask extraction**: `brew reinstall --cask claude-code`; this re-extracts the full cask and restores the binary
- **Fix — partial Homebrew state**: `brew cleanup claude-code && brew reinstall --cask claude-code` ensures stale artifacts are removed before reinstall
- **Verify resolution**: After reinstall, `claude --version` must succeed and `coding/bin/coding` should start normally in default mode
- **Distinguishing from PATH config issues**: If `which claude` resolves but `claude --version` fails with a "no such file" error, the cause is a dangling symlink, not a PATH misconfiguration

- **Failure mode — incomplete cask extraction**: A Homebrew cask upgrade updates the `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude` symlink to point at the new version directory but may fail to extract the binary into `/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/claude-code/<version>/`, leaving a dangling symlink; `coding/bin/coding` then fails to start because the resolved path does not exist
- **Manual symlink fix is insufficient**: The binary file itself is absent, not merely misdirected; correcting the symlink target without a reinstall still yields a missing file

## Key Files
- `coding/bin/coding` — agent runner entry point; requires `claude` on `PATH`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude` — Homebrew-managed symlink; inspect with `ls -la /opt/homebrew/bin/claude`
- `/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/claude-code/<version>/` — cask extraction directory; must contain the actual `claude` binary after a successful install

## Usage
`coding/bin/coding` is invoked directly to start any agent session. It must be able to exec `claude` before any session logic runs. The dependency is implicit (via `PATH`) rather than an explicit configured path.

## Troubleshooting
- **`coding/bin/coding` fails to start after a `claude-code` upgrade**: Run `ls -la $(which claude)` to check for a dangling symlink, then `ls /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/claude-code/` to confirm the binary is missing from the new version directory
- **Fix — dangling symlink after incomplete cask extraction**: `brew reinstall --cask claude-code`; this re-extracts the full cask and restores the binary
- **Fix — partial Homebrew state**: `brew cleanup claude-code && brew reinstall --cask claude-code` ensures stale artifacts are removed before reinstall
- **Verify resolution**: After reinstall, `claude --version` must succeed and `coding/bin/coding` should start normally in default mode
- **Distinguishing from PATH config issues**: If `which claude` resolves but `claude --version` fails with a "no such file" error, the cause is a dangling symlink, not a PATH misconfiguration

topic: Performance Dashboard — Experiment Controls and Stop Routing
summary:
## Purpose
Phase 74's browser UI for experiment performance reporting includes live start/stop controls for measurement runs. The Stop button triggers the full close pipeline (token aggregation + LLM judge + `Run`/`Score` write). API routing is fully established through `vkb-server`'s existing experiment endpoints; the start/stop button is a UI-only addition requiring no new server infrastructure. This feature is unanimously nominated as the first self-contained task to run through the measurement loop.

## Architecture
**Experiment lifecycle:**
- Runs stamped with `task_id` on start (format: `exp-{context}-{condition}-{model}-{run}`, e.g., `exp-dash-start-control-claude-opus-4-8`)
- Stop triggers the full close pipeline in `measurement-stop.mjs`: token aggregation → six heuristics → LLM judge → `Run`/`Score` write
- Re-runs with varied parameters (model, agent, framework) are comparable via `task_hash` grouping

**API routing (decided, no new infrastructure):**
- Dashboard start/stop routes through `vkb-server`'s `/api/experiments/*` endpoints
- `api-routes.js` in `vkb-server` is the sole wiring file — no new server process or service required
- All new code is UI-side: button components + calls to existing endpoints

**Scope boundary:**
- Server-side: no changes required; endpoints already exist
- New code: UI button components and client-side event handlers targeting the Performance tab only

## Key Files
- `api-routes.js` (in `vkb-server`) — experiment endpoint wiring; already handles start/stop routing
- `measurement-stop.mjs` — backend close pipeline invoked by the stop endpoint; no modifications required for this feature
- Performance Dashboard UI (in `vkb-server` frontend) — location for new start/stop button components

## Usage
Used by developers running controlled multi-turn experiments from the browser UI. The start/stop buttons expose the same capability as the CLI tools (`measurement-start.mjs` / `measurement-stop.mjs`) without requiring terminal access. Output feeds the same `task_hash`-grouped results view as CLI-initiated runs.

## Troubleshooting
- **Stop button not triggering scoring**: verify `/api/experiments/stop` fully delegates to `measurement-stop.mjs`; confirm `task_id` was stamped at start — missing `task_id` prevents span close
- **Results absent from `task_hash` grouping**: confirm both start and stop completed cleanly; incomplete spans are not grouped in the dashboard view
- **Partial pipeline execution**: the stop endpoint must invoke the full pipeline (token aggregation → heuristics → LLM judge); early exits silently skip scoring — check `vkb-server` logs for pipeline stage completion

summary:
## Purpose
Manages all writes to the observations and insights database and provides recovery mechanisms for missing data. Targeted gap-window backfill scripts recover observations from confirmed-empty time ranges; an async post-write resolver corrects pronoun antecedents and recovers image-stripped rows. All writes must be strictly bounded to confirmed-empty windows to prevent duplicates.

## Architecture
**`writeInsight` return contract:**
- Returns an **object `{legacyId, mintedId}`** — never a scalar `row.id`
- All callers must destructure this shape; treating the return as a scalar silently yields `undefined` for both identifiers

**`legacyId` architectural role — critical distinction:**
- `legacyId` is exclusively a **SQLite→km-core migration bridge field**; it is NOT an application-level idempotency key
- All 118 insight km-core keys are already native UUIDs; `legacyId.id` must never appear in deep-links, display logic, or any public API response
- The canonical public identifier for all insights is the **native km-core UUID `id` field**

**Idempotency matching strategy:**
- Insight upserts must match by **`topic+project`** — not by `legacyId`
- `legacyId`-based lookup as a fallback in `writeInsight` is a transitional workaround; `topic+project` is the correct long-term key
- Both deep-link breakage and duplicate-creation bugs resolve by switching all display/href logic from `legacyId.id` to native `id`

**Pending scope (unverified):**
- Whether `resynthesize` endpoints and `absorbed` tombstones correctly propagate and receive the native UUID `id` (not `legacyId`) has not been confirmed; validate before extending the UUID-display fix to those surfaces

## Key Files
- `src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js` — primary write site; `writeInsight` upsert; idempotency matching logic
- `ObservationConsolidator.js` — digest entity write site; calls `kmStore.putEntity()` and invokes `writeInsight`
- `migrate-add-project-column.js` — adds `project` column to observations table; includes dry-run validation mode

## Usage
- `writeInsight` is called by consolidation and digest pipelines to upsert per-entity insight records into the km-core store
- The return value `{legacyId, mintedId}` is consumed by callers that need to reference the created/updated insight downstream
- Native km-core UUID `id` (not `legacyId.id`) is the required identifier for Coverage tiles, deep-link hrefs, and any public API reference

## Troubleshooting
- **Duplicate insights after consolidation**: verify `writeInsight` is matching by `topic+project`; `legacyId`-based matching silently fails when `legacyId` is absent or mismatched
- **Broken deep-links on Coverage tiles**: confirm display/href logic uses `insight.id` (native UUID), not `insight.legacyId.id`
- **`mintedId` or `legacyId` is `undefined` after `writeInsight`**: caller is treating the return as a scalar; must destructure `{ legacyId, mintedId }` explicitly
- **`resynthesize` or tombstone surface receiving wrong ID**: scope not yet confirmed — probe the `:12436` endpoint to verify which ID field it consumes before extending the fix

summary:
## Purpose
Provides vector embeddings for knowledge graph lookups in km-core. The embedding client is an abstraction layer that allows different embedding backends to be swapped; `FastembedEmbeddingClient` is the current default.

## Architecture
- **Default client**: `FastembedEmbeddingClient` is the current default embedding client
- **Consumer**: `KnowledgeQueryService.js` is the primary consumer; uses embeddings to execute similarity searches against the knowledge graph
- **Phase context**: D-52 is an active sub-task sequence within Phase 42-04; the next D-52 sub-task is identified by consulting `.planning/`

## Key Files
- `KnowledgeQueryService.js` — knowledge query service; instantiates/consumes the embedding client for vector similarity lookups
- `.planning/phases/` — phase planning directory; consult for the next D-52 sub-task and Phase 42-04 scope

## Usage
Embedding is triggered whenever `KnowledgeQueryService` performs a similarity-based knowledge lookup. The default client (`FastembedEmbeddingClient`) runs locally without an external API call. Switching the default client requires updating the client factory or DI wiring consumed by `KnowledgeQueryService.js`.

## Troubleshooting
- **Embedding client not found after default switch**: confirm `FastembedEmbeddingClient` is correctly wired as the default in the client factory/instantiation path consumed by `KnowledgeQueryService.js`
- **Next D-52 sub-task unknown**: read `.planning/` (specifically the Phase 42-04 context and STATE file) to identify the next pending sub-task before writing new code
- **Slow or failing local embeddings**: `FastembedEmbeddingClient` runs a local model; verify the model file is present and the fastembed native dependency is correctly installed for the current platform

summary:
## Purpose
Renders the km-core knowledge graph as an interactive node graph with filter panels (including the Online filter for entity ancestry traces). Connects to obs-api at `:12436` for all graph data. `ApiClient.ts` mediates every fetch between the frontend and the obs-api REST layer.

## Architecture
- **Node fetch layer**: `listEntities()` in `ApiClient.ts` is the primary data fetch call; it carries a silent 1000-entity cap — when the graph exceeds 1000 nodes, the viewer renders whatever subset is returned, causing disconnected "island" artifacts where edges reference nodes that were not fetched. The cap is not surfaced as an error or warning.
- **Fix for node cap**: remove or raise the limit in `listEntities()` to request the full node set; rebuild `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/` after the change
- **Filter panels**: include the Online filter for ancestry trace display; ancestry correctness depends on both complete node fetch and correct `ontologyClass` assignments in km-core (see km-core Ontology Hierarchy Root Enforcement insight)
- **Data source**: all entity and edge data served by obs-api (`:12436`); viewer dist must be rebuilt after any `ApiClient.ts` or source change

## Key Files
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/ApiClient.ts` — `listEntities()` method; 1000-node fetch cap; primary data fetch entry point
- `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/` — compiled viewer artifacts; must be rebuilt after any source change; mtime used as rebuild confirmation signal
- obs-api at `:12436` — backend data source for all `listEntities()` and related calls

## Usage
Viewer is accessed via HTTP on its exposed Docker port. `listEntities()` is called on graph load and on filter interaction. Any knowledge graph exceeding 1000 nodes requires the cap to be removed before rendering is reliable. After modifying `ApiClient.ts`, rebuild `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/` and confirm the mtime has advanced.

## Troubleshooting
- **Disconnected node "islands" in viewer after data repair**: primary suspect is the 1000-node cap in `listEntities()` in `ApiClient.ts`; confirm node count exceeds 1000 by querying obs-api directly, then remove/raise the cap and rebuild viewer dist
- **Islands persist after cap fix**: secondary cause may be stale or race-clobbered `general.json` (see km-core multi-instance write race); verify obs-api is the sole active `GraphKMStore` writer
- **Ancestry trace truncates in Online filter**: `ontologyClass` misclassification on a root entity (e.g., CK entity tagged `"Detail"`); see km-core Ontology Hierarchy Root Enforcement insight for repair procedure
- **Viewer dist not reflecting code change**: dist must be rebuilt after any source change; verify mtime on `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/`
- **`listEntities()` returning partial dataset with no error**: silent cap is the cause — no warning or truncation signal is emitted; the only indicator is comparing returned node count against total node count from obs-api

- **Disconnected node "islands" in viewer after data repair**: primary suspect is the 1000-node cap in `listEntities()` in `ApiClient.ts`; confirm node count exceeds 1000 by querying obs-api directly, then remove/raise the cap and rebuild viewer dist
- **Islands persist after cap fix**: secondary cause may be stale or race-clobbered `general.json` (see km-core multi-instance write race); verify obs-api is the sole active `GraphKMStore` writer
- **Ancestry trace truncates in Online filter**: `ontologyClass` misclassification on a root entity (e.g., CK entity tagged `"Detail"`); see km-core Ontology Hierarchy Root Enforcement insight for repair procedure
- **Viewer dist not reflecting code change**: dist must be rebuilt after any source change; verify mtime on `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/`
- **`listEntities()` returning partial dataset with no error**: silent cap is the cause — no warning or truncation signal is emitted; the only indicator is comparing returned node count against total node count from obs-api

metadata.scope: Interactive knowledge graph viewer in `integrations/unified-viewer/`, including ApiClient.ts data fetch layer and node rendering
metadata.summary:
## Purpose
Renders the km-core knowledge graph as an interactive node graph with filter panels (including the Online filter for entity ancestry traces). Connects to obs-api at `:12436` for all graph data. `ApiClient.ts` mediates every fetch between the frontend and the obs-api REST layer.

## Architecture
- **Node fetch layer**: `listEntities()` in `ApiClient.ts` is the primary data fetch call; it carries a silent 1000-entity cap — when the graph exceeds 1000 nodes, the viewer renders whatever subset is returned, causing disconnected "island" artifacts where edges reference nodes that were not fetched. The cap is not surfaced as an error or warning.
- **Fix for node cap**: remove or raise the limit in `listEntities()` to request the full node set; rebuild `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/` after the change
- **Filter panels**: include the Online filter for ancestry trace display; ancestry correctness depends on both complete node fetch and correct `ontologyClass` assignments in km-core (see km-core Ontology Hierarchy Root Enforcement insight)
- **Data source**: all entity and edge data served by obs-api (`:12436`); viewer dist must be rebuilt after any `ApiClient.ts` or source change

## Key Files
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/ApiClient.ts` — `listEntities()` method; 1000-node fetch cap; primary data fetch entry point
- `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/` — compiled viewer artifacts; must be rebuilt after any source change; mtime used as rebuild confirmation signal
- obs-api at `:12436` — backend data source for all `listEntities()` and related calls

## Usage
Viewer is accessed via HTTP on its exposed Docker port. `listEntities()` is called on graph load and on filter interaction. Any knowledge graph exceeding 1000 nodes requires the cap to be removed before rendering is reliable. After modifying `ApiClient.ts`, rebuild `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/` and confirm the mtime has advanced.

## Troubleshooting
- **Disconnected node "islands" in viewer after data repair**: primary suspect is the 1000-node cap in `listEntities()` in `ApiClient.ts`; confirm node count exceeds 1000 by querying obs-api directly, then remove/raise the cap and rebuild viewer dist
- **Islands persist after cap fix**: secondary cause may be stale or race-clobbered `general.json` (see km-core multi-instance write race); verify obs-api is the sole active `GraphKMStore` writer
- **Ancestry trace truncates in Online filter**: `ontologyClass` misclassification on a root entity (e.g., CK entity tagged `"Detail"`); see km-core Ontology Hierarchy Root Enforcement insight for repair procedure
- **Viewer dist not reflecting code change**: dist must be rebuilt after any source change; verify mtime on `integrations/unified-viewer/dist/`
- **`listEntities()` returning partial dataset with no error**: silent cap is the cause — no warning or truncation signal is emitted; the only indicator is comparing returned node count against total node count from obs-api

summary:
## Purpose
Prevents writer agents from incorrectly overwriting the `ontologyClass` field on root-level entities (e.g., `CollectiveKnowledge`) with non-root types such as `"Detail"`. Misclassification silently truncates ancestry traces in the unified viewer's filter panels, causing the Online filter to stop at the project level rather than reaching the correct root. A one-shot repair script corrects any existing misclassification idempotently.

## Architecture
Three cooperating components:

- **`HIERARCHY_ROOTS` registry** (`lib/km-core/`): canonical exported list of top-level ontology class names. Acts as the reference set that all agents and guards consult when evaluating whether an entity is a hierarchy root.
- **Writer-side hard-root guard** (`integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis`): intercepts incoming `ontologyClass` write operations; rejects any attempt to overwrite an entity whose current class appears in `HIERARCHY_ROOTS`. Activated at runtime via bind-mount — when Docker rebuild is unavailable (e.g., blocked by corporate proxy), restart the MCP server via `supervisorctl` to load updated code without rebuilding the image.
- **Repair script** (`scripts/repair-ck-ontology-class.mjs`): idempotent one-shot script targeting the `CollectiveKnowledge` entity; operates in two modes — dry-run (lists affected entities, no writes) and live (flips `ontologyClass` from `"Detail"` to `"System"`). Both modes are safe to re-run.

**Verification criterion:** after live repair, the Online filter ancestry trace in the unified viewer for the CK entity must reach `CollectiveKnowledge` — not truncate at the project level.

## Key Files
- `lib/km-core/` — `HIERARCHY_ROOTS` registry exports; source of truth for root-class names
- `scripts/repair-ck-ontology-class.mjs` — idempotent repair script; dry-run + live modes; targets CK `ontologyClass` field
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis` — semantic-analysis agent containing the writer-side hard-root guard; activated via bind-mount + `supervisorctl` restart when Docker rebuild is blocked

## Usage
**Operator repair procedure (Plan 60-04 checkpoint):**
1. Run dry-run: inspect reported entities and confirm only the CK entity is targeted
2. Run live pass: flips `ontologyClass` from `"Detail"` to `"System"` on the CK entity
3. Visual confirmation: open the unified viewer → Online filter → verify the CK ancestry trace reaches `CollectiveKnowledge` (not truncated at project level)

The hard-root guard in the semantic-analysis agent prevents re-introduction of the misclassification going forward. All unit tests across `lib/km-core` and `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis` pass with zero regression after the guard and registry were added.

## Troubleshooting
- **Online filter ancestry truncates at the project level**: CK `ontologyClass` is likely set to `"Detail"`; run the repair script dry-run to confirm, then live pass to fix
- **Repair script re-run on already-patched entity**: the script is idempotent — safe to re-run; no duplicate changes will be written
- **Docker rebuild blocked (corporate proxy)**: do not attempt to rebuild the Docker image; restart the MCP server via `supervisorctl` to activate bind-mounted code changes (`HIERARCHY_ROOTS` export and hard-root guard)
- **Hard-root guard not preventing new misclassifications**: confirm the MCP server was restarted after the guard code was deployed; a stale process will not have the guard loaded
- **`HIERARCHY_ROOTS` export missing from km-core**: guard and repair script both depend on this export; verify the registry is exported from `lib/km-core/` before testing or running the repair script
- **Plan 60-04 worktree merge blocked**: 60-04 is an `autonomous: false` checkpoint task pinned to main-tree execution; operator must explicitly approve after verifying the dry-run output and visual ancestry trace before the orchestrator advances phase STATE

topic: Knowledge Graph Ancestry — capturedBy BFS Traversal
summary:
## Purpose
`ancestry.ts` implements BFS traversal for resolving ancestors and descendants within the knowledge graph. A known pending defect causes incorrect traversal results for `capturedBy` relationships due to an incorrect edge direction assumption in the BFS logic.

## Architecture
- **BFS traversal**: `ancestry.ts` walks graph edges in a specified direction (inbound/outbound) to resolve lineage chains; the `capturedBy` edge direction is the identified failure point
- **Raw export as ground truth**: `general.json` is the authoritative source for inspecting raw edge structures, including `capture`-type relations; `jq` is the standard tool for counting and sampling these edges
- **Edge representation**: `capture`-type relations appear in the export with inspectable structure (relation type, source, target, attributes); sampling the first several matching edges is the recommended baseline approach before modifying traversal logic
- **Schema caution**: the `.Class` field does not exist in the KG export; always sample a raw record to confirm actual field names before writing filter expressions
- **VKB surface**: `captured_by` is one of the five actual edge types rendered in the VKB graph viewer (`parent_child`, `related_to`, `has_insight`, `capturedBy`, `contains`); the traversal bug may affect VKB-level query correctness

## Key Files
- `ancestry.ts` — BFS traversal implementation; `capturedBy` edge direction is a confirmed pending fix; exact path to be confirmed from project source layout
- `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json` — raw edge export; query with `jq` to count and sample `capture`-type relations before applying the direction fix

## Usage
`ancestry.ts` is invoked wherever the knowledge graph must resolve the lineage of a node. The pending `capturedBy` direction fix must be preceded by a `jq`-based audit of `general.json` to confirm the actual source/target orientation of `capture` edges, ensuring the fix corrects to the canonical direction rather than inverting to a different wrong direction.

## Troubleshooting
- **`capturedBy` traversal returns wrong ancestors or descendants**: BFS edge direction for `capturedBy` in `ancestry.ts` is a confirmed pending defect; verify canonical edge orientation by querying `general.json` with `jq` (adapt field names from a sampled raw record — `.Class` does not exist in the schema) before applying the fix
- **Uncertain which field name the export uses for relation type**: sample the first raw edge object (`jq '.[<edge-collection-key>][0]'`) to inspect all top-level keys before writing filter expressions
- **`capturedBy` fix interacts with VKB viewer rendering**: `captured_by` is a rendered edge type in the VKB viewer; after fixing traversal direction, verify that viewer-level edge display and legend entries still reflect correct directionality

metadata.scope: `ancestry.ts` BFS traversal logic, with specific focus on the pending `capturedBy` edge direction defect

summary:
## Purpose
Manages reliable startup and restart of host-side services (obs_api, coordinators, etc.), handling port-binding races and providing diagnostic data for unexpected process termination.

## Architecture
- **EADDRINUSE retry**: A `waitForPortBindable` helper implements retry logic for cases not covered by the existing restart helper, polling until a port is free before attempting to bind
- **SIGTERM diagnostics**: `process.ppid` is logged alongside SIGTERM events to enable cheap tracing of which parent process sent the signal — useful for diagnosing unexpected service terminations
- **Restart helper**: Existing restart logic handles the common crash/restart path; `waitForPortBindable` covers the gap where a port is still occupied from a previous run during startup

## Key Files
- `scripts/start-services-robust.js` — service orchestrator; `waitForPortBindable` helper; SIGTERM log with `process.ppid`

## Usage
- Invoked to start the suite of host-side agentic services; ensures services do not race on port binding after restarts
- When `obs_api` appears to crash and recover (visible as a new PID with ~1min uptime), check SIGTERM log entries with `ppid` to identify the signal source

## Troubleshooting
- **Service fails to start with EADDRINUSE**: Previous instance did not fully release the port; `waitForPortBindable` should handle this automatically — if not, verify it is called before the bind attempt
- **Unexpected SIGTERM on obs_api**: Check service logs for the SIGTERM entry and use the logged `ppid` to identify the sending process (launchd, shell, or another orchestrator instance)

topic: Dashboard Dark-Mode and Color Token System
summary:
## Purpose
The dashboard UI implements a dark-mode theme through CSS custom properties defined in `src/index.css`. A subset of color references in `constants.ts` use hardcoded hex values that bypass the CSS variable system entirely, causing those elements to fail to adapt when dark mode is active. A central color-token refactor is the planned remediation.

## Architecture
**Theme layer (functional):**
- `src/index.css` defines CSS custom properties for colors, backgrounds, borders, and fills
- Dark-mode retrofit is committed and verified in the compiled CSS bundle
- Components that reference these custom properties adapt automatically on theme toggle

**Hardcoded bypass (open issue):**
- `constants.ts` contains color definitions as raw hex values (e.g., chart fill colors, stroke values)
- These constants are consumed directly by components — no CSS variable resolution occurs at render time
- Components fed by `constants.ts` fills do not respond to dark-mode switching and render with light-mode colors regardless of theme state

**Planned refactor:**
- Migrate all hardcoded hex values in `constants.ts` to reference CSS custom properties/design tokens defined in `src/index.css`
- Until complete, dark-mode rendering is partially correct: theme-aware components adapt; constants-sourced components do not

## Key Files
- `src/index.css` — CSS custom property definitions; primary theme variable source
- `constants.ts` — hardcoded hex fill/color values that bypass the theme (target of pending color-token refactor)

## Usage
- Dark-mode activates the CSS variable set via browser preference or explicit theme toggle
- Correctness of dark-mode rendering is verified by browser inspection: toggle theme and observe whether fill/stroke colors update

## Troubleshooting
- **Elements remain light-colored in dark mode**: Trace the fill/color source for the component — if sourced from `constants.ts`, the color-token refactor is required for that element
- **CSS bundle not reflecting committed theme changes**: The dashboard `dist/` is NOT auto-rebuilt on commit; run `npm run build` explicitly after modifying `src/index.css` or related source files
- **Verify theme variable activation**: In browser DevTools, inspect that CSS custom properties (e.g., `--background`, `--foreground`) reflect dark-mode values when dark mode is active

topic: Health Coordinator — Phase 33 Consolidated Health Monitoring
summary:
## Purpose
Consolidates all service probing into a single coordinator process exposing health results and system-wide state over HTTP. Eliminates split-brain between consumers and provides auto-healing for Proxydetox and the LLM proxy through a cascaded FSM. The coordinator is the authoritative health source; downstream services must not independently probe LLM or network endpoints.

## Architecture
- **Coordinator daemon**: `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — Express server on port **3034**; managed via LaunchAgent plist; ~5s tick for semantic probe scheduling; full network/proxy probe cycle runs at a longer reduced-frequency interval.
- **Port 3033 ownership (critical)**: Port 3033 is owned by the `coding-services` Docker container as a port-forward; the coordinator listens on **3034** only — never bind or query coordinator state on 3033.
- **Monitored endpoints**: `api.github.com`, `api.anthropic.com`, `localhost:12435` (LLM proxy), corporate DNS host.
- **Auto-spawner** (`health-coordinator.js` lines 1485–1504): spawns one launchd-managed ETM job per detected active project. **Current limitation**: gates exclusively on Claude `.jsonl` transcript freshness — OpenCode `.db` sessions are not recognized, so RA/OpenCode-only projects are excluded from auto-spawn. See *Health Coordinator / Tmux Statusline — ETM Spawning and Project Detection* for full detail and pending fixes.
- **Dashboard "Degraded" state**: triggered when any critical monitored endpoint is unreachable or unresponsive — including when the LLM proxy process is alive and port-bound but not actively servicing requests.

## Key Files
- `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — coordinator daemon; probe scheduling; cascaded FSM; auto-spawner (lines 1485–1504)
- `scripts/combined-status-line.js` — primary consumer; fetches all health state from coordinator on port 3034
- LaunchAgent plist — manages coordinator lifecycle (auto-restart, environment variables)

## Usage
All health consumers (tmux statusline, dashboard UI, LSL badges) query the coordinator exclusively at port 3034. The coordinator runs continuously as a LaunchAgent. Downstream services must not implement independent probes of LLM or network endpoints.

## Troubleshooting
- **Dashboard "Degraded" with LLM proxy PID alive**: a process can hold port 12435 while not servicing requests — PID liveness does not equal request-handling health. Kill and restart the proxy process.
- **RA/OpenCode project absent from auto-spawn**: auto-spawner only checks Claude `.jsonl` freshness; OpenCode `.db` sessions are not yet a recognized signal. Manually start the RA ETM via launchd as a workaround.
- **Sub-checks showing 'unknown'**: restart the coordinator to force a full re-probe cycle.
- **Port conflict on 3033**: coordinator must never bind to 3033 (owned by Docker); wrong-port binding causes silent mis-routing of all health queries with no obvious error.

topic: Knowledge Context Injection — Retrieval Relevance and Deduplication
summary:
## Purpose
Ensures that the knowledge injected into agent prompts is topically relevant, non-redundant, and favors recently-verified insights — addressing historically poor signal-to-noise ratios caused by compressed MiniLM embedding scores, broken dedup logic, and absence of freshness weighting.

## Architecture
- **Embedding similarity compression**: MiniLM cosine scores cluster in the 0.75–0.82 range, making topic discrimination unreliable by score alone; Qdrant topic pre-filtering is the primary relevance gate
- **Topic pre-filtering**: `knowledge-injection-hook.js` extracts topics from the current conversation transcript and passes them to the retrieval service to pre-filter Qdrant results before RRF fusion
- **Reranking pipeline** (in `src/retrieval/retrieval-service.js`):
  1. `_applyContextBoost` — existing context-relevance boost step
  2. `_applyFreshnessRerank` — **newly added**; applies freshness decay (recency of last insight verification) and topic relevance scoring; stale or off-topic insights rank lower regardless of raw similarity score
- **Known gap**: `verificationRatio` (per-insight correctness score) does not yet influence retrieval ranking or confidence decay; `_applyFreshnessRerank` addresses recency but not accuracy weighting
- **Near-duplicate dedup** (injection-time, via `token-budget.js`): pairwise cosine similarity at **≥ 0.93** threshold before injecting insights; MiniLM cosine floor is ~0.89–0.92, making 0.90 too aggressive
- **Digest dedup**: `scripts/dedup-digests-by-embedding.js` at **0.97** threshold (high threshold required due to shared project vocabulary causing false positives at lower values)
- **Insight dedup**: `scripts/dedup-insights-by-embedding.js` — greedy pairwise merging at **≥ 0.93** (not single-linkage chaining, which causes transitive merges of unrelated items)
- **One-time cleanup results**: insights reduced 19 → 16; digests reduced 298 → 251

## Key Files
- `src/retrieval/retrieval-service.js` — reranking pipeline; `_applyContextBoost` followed by `_applyFreshnessRerank` (freshness decay + topic relevance)
- `knowledge-injection-hook.js` — topic extraction and Qdrant pre-filter call
- `token-budget.js` — per-tier caps and near-duplicate dedup at injection time (0.93 threshold for insights)
- `scripts/dedup-insights-by-embedding.js` — on-demand insight dedup; greedy pairwise merging
- `scripts/dedup-digests-by-embedding.js` — on-demand digest dedup; 0.97 threshold

## Usage
Topic filtering and freshness reranking run automatically on every hook invocation. `token-budget.js` dedup fires at injection time on every session start. Dedup scripts are run on-demand against the live DB when duplicate accumulation is suspected (after bulk consolidation runs or large data imports).

## Troubleshooting
- **Same insight injected twice at different confidence scores**: `token-budget.js` near-duplicate dedup not active or threshold too low — verify 0.93 threshold is in effect
- **Stale insights ranking above recently-verified ones**: `_applyFreshnessRerank` not wired or verification timestamps not being written to insight records — confirm the method is called after `_applyContextBoost` in `retrieval-service.js`
- **Unrelated insights merged after running dedup script**: Script using single-linkage chaining — switch to greedy pairwise merging in `dedup-insights-by-embedding.js`
- **Threshold 0.90 merging non-duplicates**: MiniLM cosine floor is 0.89–0.92; use ≥ 0.93 for insights and 0.97 for digests
- **Noise still high after topic filtering**: Qdrant may be non-functional (falling back to keyword search) — verify Qdrant health and container status; check retrieval service logs on port 3033/3848
- **`verificationRatio` not influencing results**: Accuracy-weighted ranking is a known remaining gap; `_applyFreshnessRerank` covers recency only — `verificationRatio` weighting is a future improvement

topic: Combined Status Line — tmux Session Health Display
summary:
## Purpose
`combined-status-line.js` is the sole rendering layer for all tmux `status-right` badge content: per-session health indicators, multi-project labels, graduated lifecycle icons, LSL tranche badges (`[📋]`), global health status, and the `[📚]` online-learning badge. No other module renders tmux `status-right` output.

## Architecture
**Live data pipeline:**
- **Health data**: `getGlobalHealthStatus()` fetches from the health coordinator at port 3034; all health indicators fall to red/fallback if the coordinator is unreachable.
- **Project LSL badges**: reads `lsl_by_project` from coordinator `/health/state`; a missing project entry triggers a strict "no entry → down" fallback, rendering `LSL🔴` and suppressing the `[PROJECT]` label simultaneously — this is a rendering consequence of a coordinator/daemon gap, not a bug in this file.
- **Transcript freshness**: `transcriptAgeMs()` scans Claude `.jsonl` files and OpenCode `.specstory/history/` markdown files, picking the freshest.
- **Tranche time remaining**: `calculateTimeRemaining()` reconstructs the tranche end time from start + duration. Midnight-crossing detection: when the computed end-of-window is earlier than the start-of-window, the end time is rolled forward by 24 hours before computing remaining minutes. This is the **only** function in this file performing time-of-day reconstruction; no other badge builder (health, constraint, knowledge, proxy, network, semantic) performs this calculation, so the midnight-crossing fix is fully isolated here.

## Key Files
- `combined-status-line.js` — sole file for all status-right rendering; `calculateTimeRemaining()` is the only locus of time-of-day math

## Usage
Invoked by tmux on each poll interval to render the `status-right` string. Consumes the health coordinator's HTTP endpoints (`:3034`) and local filesystem state (transcript `.jsonl` and `.specstory/history/` files).

## Troubleshooting
- **`LSL🔴` or missing `[PROJECT]` label**: the coordinator holds no heartbeat entry for that project; investigate the responsible ETM daemon and coordinator registration, not this file.
- **Tranche badge shows `[📋🔴...(ended)]` during 23:00–00:00**: midnight-crossing detection in `calculateTimeRemaining()` covers this case; verify the end < start condition check and 24-hour rollover arithmetic are intact.
- **All badges stale/red simultaneously**: coordinator unreachable on port 3034; restart the coordinator daemon.
- **`[📚]` badge stale**: staleness originates in `ObservationConsolidator.js` `lastObservationAt` — not in this file; see Online Learning insight.

topic: GSM Health Warning and Statusline Rendering
summary:
## Purpose
Renders a real-time health summary in the tmux status bar for all active projects, showing emoji badges for coordinator state, knowledge pipeline, LSL health, and system warnings. Degraded state is surfaced on the next refresh cycle.

## Architecture
- **Rendering chain**: tmux config → `status-line-fast.cjs` (fast path with cache borrow) → `combined-status-line.js` (full render)
- **Fast path**: `status-line-fast.cjs` borrows a sibling cache file filtered by `-w${paneWidth}.txt` suffix to avoid cross-width contamination; cache TTL is **30s**; safety timer is **10s** (deliberately exceeds CSL's internal 8s timeout so CSL can emit its own `⚠️ SYS:TIMEOUT` badge rather than being silently killed)
- **Cascade protection**: `getCoordinatorState` in `combined-status-line.js` is hardened against transient `curl` failures to `:3034/health/state` — a single failed probe must not degrade all coordinator-derived badges simultaneously
- **Cell-width residue buffer**: A `CELL_DRIFT_BUFFER` of +2 cells compensates for Unicode/emoji width discrepancies that can cause statusline truncation or overflow

## Key Files
- `status-line-fast.cjs` — fast-path renderer; pane-width-aware cache selection; 30s TTL; 10s safety timer
- `combined-status-line.js` — full renderer; coordinator state polling via `:3034/health/state`; cascade protection in `getCoordinatorState`

## Usage
Invoked by tmux's `status-right`/`status-left` on every refresh. `status-line-fast.cjs` serves from cache when fresh; on miss or expiry it shells out to `combined-status-line.js`. Output is a UTF-8 emoji badge string consumed directly by tmux.

## Troubleshooting
- **All badges degraded simultaneously**: Likely a transient `curl` failure — cascade protection in `getCoordinatorState` should prevent this; verify the hardening logic is intact.
- **Statusline width overflow or truncation**: Adjust `CELL_DRIFT_BUFFER`; confirm cache files are keyed on correct pane width suffix.
- **`⚠️ SYS:TIMEOUT` badge**: CSL exceeded its 8s internal timeout; check coordinator responsiveness at `:3034/health/state`.
- **Stale cache served across pane widths**: Verify cache file naming uses the `-w${paneWidth}.txt` suffix convention.

summary:
## Purpose
Constructs, enriches, and normalizes route-attribution records for Claude, Copilot, and OpenCode agents. Trace readers materialize per-call routing metadata into typed `RouteEvent` objects; a heuristic layer and goal-sentence extractor enrich those objects for downstream attribution pipeline consumption without invoking any LLM.

## Architecture
**Two cooperating layers:**

**Trace reader layer** — builds typed `RouteEvent` records from per-provider call context:
- **`buildClaudeRouteTrace`** — builds a typed route-trace record from Claude agent call context
- **`buildCopilotRouteTrace`** — builds a route-trace record from Copilot call context; implements **dual-field tolerance**: accepts either `toolName` or `name` as the tool identifier, matching Phase-69 fixture variants where both appear in real call data
- **OpenCode SQLite reader** — read-only reader extracting route trace data from the SQLite-backed OpenCode call log; shares existing WAL/uid gates via additive exports; must maintain byte-for-byte alignment with the backfill timestamp predicate
- **`buildNormalizedTrace`** — dispatcher that unifies all provider traces into a single dominant-agent output with span-window filtering

**Attribution computation layer** — enriches `RouteEvent` records with derived signals (no LLM calls):
- **`RouteEvent` schema** — typed schema defining the structure of all route-trace records produced by the reader layer; 15/15 test coverage
- **`computeHeuristics`** — derives attribution heuristics from `RouteEvent` data; pure computation, no external I/O
- **`deriveGoalSentence`** — zero-LLM extractor that infers a human-readable goal sentence using pure filesystem traversal and regex; produces zero console output; 10/10 test coverage

## Key Files
- Plan `72-01` artifacts (`.planning/phases/72-*-can/`) — implements `RouteEvent` schema and `computeHeuristics`
- Plan `72-02` artifacts (`.planning/phases/72-*-can/`) — implements `deriveGoalSentence` extractor
- (Resolved source paths are in per-plan `SUMMARY.md` files within the phase context directory)

## Usage
The trace reader layer is invoked by the attribution pipeline per agent call to populate `RouteEvent` records. `buildNormalizedTrace` is the single consumer-facing entry point for unified multi-provider trace output. `computeHeuristics` and `deriveGoalSentence` are called downstream of trace construction to enrich records before storage or reporting.

## Troubleshooting
- **Copilot trace field mismatch**: If `buildCopilotRouteTrace` produces empty tool identifiers, check whether call data supplies `toolName` or `name` — dual-field tolerance handles both, but only one will be populated per fixture variant.
- **OpenCode reader timestamp drift**: Any schema migration to the OpenCode call log requires re-verifying byte-for-byte alignment with the backfill timestamp predicate.
- **`deriveGoalSentence` producing console output**: This component is designed for zero stdout/stderr; any output is a regression. Run the 10-test baseline suite to isolate the source.
- **`RouteEvent` schema validation failures**: All trace builder outputs must conform to the `RouteEvent` schema; mismatches surface as failures in the 15-test suite for plan 72-01.

topic: System Health Dashboard — Frontend Pages and Service Architecture
summary:
## Purpose
Browser-based dashboard providing live views of raw observations, daily digests, weekly insights, token usage, coverage verification tiles, and system health cards. All data is sourced from host-side services through the port-3033 API backend. Frontend runs as a Next.js application on port 3030.

## Architecture
**Page tiers and refresh behavior:**
- **Observations page** (`src/pages/observations.tsx`): uses **two coexisting refresh mechanisms** — both are required; removing either leaves a staleness gap:
  1. A 30-second `setInterval` loop for continuous background polling
  2. `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', refetch)` — fires an immediate refetch whenever `document.visibilityState` returns to `"visible"`; prevents stale data after laptop sleep or background-tab timer throttling (browsers freeze/suppress timers in hidden tabs)
- **Sub-agent badge** (`src/components/observation-card.tsx`): renders an amber `↳ sub-agent` label on nested-agent observation rows
- **Coverage page** (`src/pages/coverage.tsx`): visualizes per-project claim verification status; deep-links use the native km-core UUID `id` as the canonical public insight identifier — never `legacyId`
- **Token usage page** (`token-usage.tsx`): recharts area/line chart with `hoursWindow` time-range selector and Top Consumers table; backend `/api/token-usage/summary` returns `avg_latency` on every `by_model` entry

**Service layers:**
- **Frontend**: Next.js on port 3030
- **API backend**: port 3033; proxies all data requests to host-side services
- **Static assets** (`integrations/system-health-dashboard/dist/`): served via live-mounted Docker volume; **not auto-rebuilt** — committed source changes require an explicit `npm run build` before reaching the running app

## Key Files
- `src/pages/observations.tsx` — observations list; contains both `setInterval` and `visibilitychange` refresh logic
- `src/components/observation-card.tsx` — observation card renderer; sub-agent badge logic
- `src/pages/coverage.tsx` — coverage page; all tile, legend, project-header badge, and deep-link `href` logic co-located here
- `token-usage.tsx` — token usage chart and Top Consumers table
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/dist/` — compiled static output; must be rebuilt after any frontend change

## Usage
Accessed via browser at `localhost:3030`. The observations page auto-refreshes every 30 seconds and additionally on tab re-focus or wake from sleep. Coverage tiles deep-link to individual insight records using the km-core UUID `id` field. Port 3033 API backend is a hard dependency; the dashboard is non-functional without it.

## Troubleshooting
- **Stale observations after sleep or tab background**: verify `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', refetch)` is present in `observations.tsx`; the 30-second timer alone is insufficient because browsers throttle backgrounded timers
- **Frontend changes not appearing in the running app**: run `npm run build` inside `integrations/system-health-dashboard/`; the Docker volume serves `dist/` which is never auto-rebuilt
- **Coverage tile deep-links broken or returning wrong record**: confirm `href` uses the km-core UUID `id` field, not `legacyId` (a migration bridge field, not a public identifier)
- **Token usage chart empty**: verify `/api/token-usage/summary?hours=N` responds on port 3033; if the backend launchd service is down, restart via `launchctl kickstart -k <service>`

topic: Startup Script and Container Health Check
summary:
## Purpose
Provides a robust container launch sequence for the `coding` CLI and related agents, handling Docker port conflicts, stale container recovery, and health-check waiting.

## Architecture
- `_container_has_unbound_ports()` — detects stale containers with ports occupied by host processes (e.g., a local Next.js dev server on :3030 blocking Docker port publishing)
- `_recover_stale_container()` — force-recreates the container when unbound ports are detected, before entering the health-wait loop
- `_diagnose_unhealthy_services` — returns exit codes (not `exit 0`) for proper error propagation
- **`free-coding-ports.sh`**: host-side utility that identifies and kills processes holding ports needed by the container (e.g., :3030); must be invoked on the host **before** `docker-compose up` — `entrypoint.sh` runs inside the container and cannot intercept host-side port-binding failures
- Health-wait timeout: **30s** (reduced from 60s)
- Host-side health check endpoint: `localhost:8080`

## Key Files
- `scripts/launch-agent-common.sh` — container launch, port conflict detection, stale container recovery, health wait loop
- `free-coding-ports.sh` — host-side port liberation script; kills processes on ports Docker needs

## Usage
`launch-agent-common.sh` is sourced by agent-specific launch scripts and runs automatically when the `coding` CLI starts a session. `free-coding-ports.sh` is invoked by the coordinator or manually when a port conflict is suspected.

## Troubleshooting
- **`coding-services` container stays in `Created` state (never starts)**: A host process is holding port 3030 — run `free-coding-ports.sh` to kill it, then start the container; use `lsof -i :3030` to identify the process manually
- **Health check timeout after Docker crash**: Stale container with unbound ports — the script auto-detects and force-recreates; if still failing, run `free-coding-ports.sh` explicitly
- **`_diagnose_unhealthy_services` not reporting correctly**: Ensure it returns exit codes and does not call `exit 0` directly

metadata.topic: Startup Script and Container Health Check

summary:
## Purpose
Provides a three-layer entity deduplication pipeline (`JaccardNameMatcher → CosineEmbeddingMatcher → LLMSemanticMatcher`) and an `IngestPipeline` orchestrator that chains extract → synthesize → dedup → store stages within the `km-core` library. Satisfies requirements PIPE-01 and DEDUP-01.

## Architecture

**Type contracts:**
- `src/dedup/types.ts` — layer contracts (`Deduplicator`, `DedupResult`, etc.)
- `src/pipeline/types.ts` — pipeline interfaces; imports from `dedup/types.ts`
- `tests/unit/_helpers/fakes.ts` — shared test helpers using direct subpath imports (types not yet re-exported via `index.ts` at time of creation)

**Three dedup layers** (all in `src/dedup/`):
- `JaccardNameMatcher.ts` — Jaccard name similarity; configurable threshold, defaults to 0.85
- `CosineEmbeddingMatcher.ts` — cosine embedding similarity; configurable threshold, defaults to 0.90
- `LLMSemanticMatcher.ts` — LLM-based semantic matching; exports `LLMDedupParseError`; `parseDedupResponse` returns discriminated union `{ ok: true, value } | { ok: false }` to distinguish parse failures from canonical empty responses without emitting false stderr warnings
- All three layers: dead self-id guard conditions removed (CR-02); self-match now returns `matched: true, confidence: 1.0`

**`LayeredDeduplicator`** (`src/dedup/LayeredDeduplicator.ts`):
- Chains the three layers in order with configurable `shortCircuit` behavior (Decision D-44)
- Short-circuits downstream layers when an earlier layer produces a definitive match

**`IngestPipeline`** (`src/pipeline/IngestPipeline.ts`):
- `runStage('extract' | 'synthesize' | 'dedup' | 'store', opts)` with 4-overload signatures (collapsed to single lines for grep-acceptance compliance)
- `opts.domain` propagated through all `runStage` calls (CR-04 fix)
- Emits `stderr` warn when entity lacks `ontologyClass` (CR-01 fix); does not silently write
- Documented deviation: `deduplicator.dedup` call returns 2 matches (not spec's expected 1) due to `runStage('dedup')` delegation architecture; noted in `40-SUMMARY.md`

**Gap closures** (Plans 40-08 through 40-11):
- CR-01: `ontologyClass` guard in `IngestPipeline.ts`
- CR-02: self-id guard removal across all 3 matchers
- CR-03: typed `LLMDedupParseError` + discriminated-union `parseDedupResponse` in `LLMSemanticMatcher.ts`
- CR-04: `opts.domain` propagation through `runStage`
- SC#1: `examples/custom-adapter.ts` — downstream reference adapter implementing all 4 stage interfaces with thresholds (0.85, 0.90, 0.70); imports only from `@fwornle/km-core`; resolved locally via `node_modules` symlink (gitignored)

**Test suite:** 153 tests passing across 17 test files after gap closure; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

## Key Files
- `src/dedup/types.ts` — layer type contracts
- `src/pipeline/types.ts` — pipeline interfaces
- `src/dedup/JaccardNameMatcher.ts` — Jaccard layer; threshold 0.85
- `src/dedup/CosineEmbeddingMatcher.ts` — cosine embedding layer; threshold 0.90
- `src/dedup/LLMSemanticMatcher.ts` — LLM semantic layer; `LLMDedupParseError`; discriminated-union parse
- `src/dedup/LayeredDeduplicator.ts` — chain orchestrator; `shortCircuit` option
- `src/pipeline/IngestPipeline.ts` — pipeline; `runStage` 4-overload; CR-01 and CR-04 fixes
- `tests/unit/_helpers/fakes.ts` — shared test helpers
- `examples/custom-adapter.ts` — SC#1 reference adapter
- `.planning/phases/40-ingest-pipeline-layered-dedup/` — 11 plan files (40-01 through 40-11), `40-REVIEW.md`, `40-VERIFICATION.md`

## Usage
Construct `IngestPipeline` with extractor, synthesizer, embedding client, LLM client, and optional dedup thresholds. Call `ingest(entities)` for the full pipeline or `runStage(stageName, opts)` for explicit stage control. SC#4 (co-exist mode second half) is deferred to Phase 42 per Decision D-45. Phase 42 imports this pipeline via the km-core root barrel.

## Troubleshooting
- **Self-match returns `matched: false`**: Dead self-id guard still present — CR-02 removes it; self-match must return `matched: true, confidence: 1.0` for all three matchers
- **LLM parse error spam in stderr for empty responses**: `parseDedupResponse` using old `canonicalEmptyResponse` pattern — verify discriminated-union return type is in place in `LLMSemanticMatcher.ts`
- **`opts.domain` silently dropped**: CR-04 not applied — verify every `runStage` call in `IngestPipeline.ts` forwards `opts.domain`
- **Entity silently skipped with no warning**: CR-01 guard missing — `IngestPipeline.ts` must emit `stderr` warn when `ontologyClass` is absent
- **Custom adapter `@fwornle/km-core` import fails**: `node_modules/@fwornle/km-core` symlink absent — create it pointing to the km-core source directory (equivalent to `npm link`; must be gitignored)
- **`deduplicator.dedup` count unexpected**: Documented deviation — `runStage('dedup')` delegation returns 2, not 1; behavior is correct and documented in `40-SUMMARY.md`
- **grep fails on `runStage` overload signatures**: Multi-line overloads not accepted — collapse each overload signature to a single line

topic: Rapid-LLM-Proxy (RLP) Architecture and CLI Subprocess Behavior
summary:
## Purpose
Routes LLM requests from all downstream coding agents through provider-specific paths. Manages per-process provider selection, model-name normalization, per-call telemetry, and agent attribution. A third endpoint extends the proxy for foreground-task `task_id` token capture via an Anthropic-native passthrough.

## Architecture

**`POST /api/complete`** — primary legacy endpoint:
- Non-streaming path: uses Copilot HTTP fallback (reliable)
- Streaming path on claude-code OAuth: Copilot HTTP fallback is structurally absent → hard 400s propagate uncaught with no recovery path
- `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` clients always send `stream: true` and consistently hit the broken streaming path when routed to claude-code

**`POST /v1/chat/completions`** (`_shimOpenAI` — OpenCode shim):
- Stamps `agent='opencode'` on all calls; normalizes OpenCode request format to internal schema
- `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` clients always send `stream: true` → same broken streaming path when provider is claude-code

**`POST /v1/messages`** (ATTR-05 proxy bridge):
- Anthropic-native passthrough for foreground-task token capture
- Streams the upstream Anthropic response verbatim — no buffering or modification
- On stream close: extracts the `usage` metadata block and writes a `token_usage` row stamped with the active `task_id`
- Auth: caller's Max OAuth bearer token forwarded directly to Anthropic — no token translation required
- Endpoint hunk at `server.mjs` lines 1585–1641

## Key Files
- `server.mjs` — all three endpoints; routing logic, provider dispatch, `task_id` resolution; `/v1/messages` endpoint hunk at lines 1585–1641
- `src/token-usage.ts` — ATTR-05 proxy bridge: `task_id` resolution and `token_usage` row write path consumed by `/v1/messages`

## Usage
All four coding agents (`claude`, `copilot`, `opencode`, `mastra`) route through this proxy via the `coding/bin/coding` launcher injection. The `/v1/messages` endpoint is specifically targeted by foreground Claude instances and `Task`-tool sub-agents whose `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is set to the proxy address.

## Troubleshooting
- **Hard 400s on streaming with claude-code**: The Copilot HTTP fallback is absent for the streaming path in `/api/complete`. `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` always streams — route these clients through a non-claude-code provider or repair the streaming fallback.
- **`token_usage` rows missing for foreground tasks**: Verify `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` points to the proxy for the calling agent. `Task`-tool sub-agents may not inherit this env-var — this is the primary open risk for the `/v1/messages` endpoint.
- **`task_id` missing on write**: Inspect `task_id` resolution logic in `server.mjs` lines 1585–1641 and `src/token-usage.ts`; confirm the active task context is propagated into the stream-close handler.
- **Stream-close handler not firing**: Check for half-open connections; the `token_usage` write depends entirely on the stream-close event firing reliably after each response.
